scope: Full source-bound visual system, decision spine, responsive composition, attention, Human-QA and falsification control.
findings: The first screen names category, audience and public value, uses an owned product surface as proof and keeps catalog before auth.
Five chapters add orientation, mechanism, product proof, public routes and an earned handoff without empty repetition.
Desktop, mobile and exact 320 preserve a project-specific visual system, readable hierarchy and zero horizontal overflow.
No-JS and failed-script paths fail closed; blind Human-QA v4 understood the public-before-auth route without friction.
The visually credible auth-first control is rejected by decision gates, so visual polish cannot hide the wrong product route.
residuals: Human-QA and attention audit are expert proxies, not participant research, eye tracking or conversion evidence.
Post-auth publication is outside the owned runtime proof.
Volatile catalog evidence requires refresh or expiry before later releases.
Cross-browser, real-device and post-deploy behavior remain separate release checks.
Specific Onest binaries do not yet have a separate provenance receipt.
